seo_home_title: Mimir - open-source local RAG for sensitive documents
seo_home_description: Mimir is an open-source CLI, library, and MCP server for sovereign local retrieval over confidential documents.
seo_home_keywords: Mimir, local RAG, sovereign RAG, private AI, confidential documents, MCP server, open-source RAG, local-first retrieval
seo_image_alt: Mimir open-source local RAG for confidential documents
hero_badge: Open-source local RAG. CLI, library, MCP.
hero_description: Mimir indexes confidential files inside the repository or workspace you choose. Agents can search and ask over local evidence without sending the raw document set to a hosted knowledge base.
terminal_install_prompt: Choose the install command that matches your workspace.
terminal_setup_output: agent kit installed - local index ready
terminal_ask_command: pnpm exec mimir ask "Which evidence proves local-only retrieval?"
proof_local_text: Documents, indexes, redaction, access logs, and generated state stay in local project folders.
proof_cited_text: Mimir returns cited passages that your agent or model can synthesize from, instead of hiding sources.
proof_agent_text: Claude, Codex, Kimi, OpenCode, Cline, or your own tool can call the same bounded local retrieval server.
library_title: Install it where the sensitive files already live.
library_text: Mimir is a public MIT package. Use it as a CLI for a repository, import it as a TypeScript library, or expose it as an MCP server for trusted agents.
library_search_command: pnpm exec mimir search "contract renewal risk"
desktop_title: A visual client is coming after the core is solid.
desktop_text: The Tauri desktop app will wrap the same local-first engine for non-CLI workflows. It is planned for direct downloads and sideloadable installers, not app stores.
agents_title: Give Claude, Codex, Kimi and local tools the same cited retrieval layer.
agents_text: Mimir installs agent-specific MCP helpers from the same local project state. You choose the workspace, install only the agents you use, and keep generated files ignored by default.
agents_steps_title: Three commands, one local source of truth.
agents_steps_text: Start in the repository that owns the documents. Mimir writes local helper files instead of asking you to copy credentials into a hosted service.
agents_step_install_text: Add Mimir to the workspace so the CLI and MCP server run beside the documents.
agents_step_setup_text: Generate ignored project state, default config, and agent helper files.
agents_step_connect_title: Install only the agents you use
agents_step_connect_text: Target Claude, Codex, Kimi, OpenCode, Cline, or a narrower subset.
agents_claude_text: Project-scoped skill and MCP helper files for local retrieval during Claude Code sessions.
agents_codex_text: Codex config helpers keep retrieval bounded to the selected repository instead of a global corpus.
agents_kimi_text: Kimi receives the same local MCP entrypoint, so evidence stays consistent across agents.
agents_other_text: OpenCode, Cline, and custom MCP clients can use the same generated local server contract.
use_cases_title: Use Mimir when the source material should not become someone else's dataset.
use_cases_text: The first value is simple: keep private documents local, then give agents a precise way to retrieve grounded evidence.
use_case_legal_text: Index contracts, tax notes, incorporation memos, or residency research and ask for the cited source behind each claim.
use_case_rfp_text: Search tender files, security requirements, and evidence packs without uploading the dossier to a vendor search service.
use_case_company_text: Expose policies, runbooks, decisions, and project notes through MCP while keeping access scoped to the local workspace.
use_case_research_text: Prepare briefings, Markdown reports, and audio summaries from local sources while separating evidence from inference.
faq_title: What Mimir does and does not send away.
faq_text: The public package is useful now, while the desktop client stays in development.
faq_private_question: Does Mimir upload my confidential documents?
faq_private_answer: No. Mimir indexes the local folders you choose and keeps generated state under ignored project folders such as .kb/ and .mimir/. Payment and future license flows must stay metadata-only.
faq_formats_question: Can it index every file format?
faq_formats_answer: No. Mimir supports common text, PDF, Office, OpenDocument, EPUB, HTML, JSON and YAML paths, and reports unsupported files explicitly. Scanned PDFs need an opt-in OCR command.
faq_desktop_question: Is the desktop app available now?
faq_desktop_answer: Not yet. The open-source CLI, library and MCP server are the current product foundation. The Tauri desktop client is planned for direct downloads and sideloadable installers after native release gates are ready.
desktop_teaser_title: The desktop client will make local retrieval visible without moving the data.
desktop_teaser_text: The app is intentionally behind the core package. The target is a direct-download Tauri client for selecting local projects, watching folders, and running the same Mimir commands through a visual workflow.
desktop_teaser_mock_text: No store dependency, no hosted document sync, no placeholder download claim before release gates are real.
desktop_teaser_workspace_text: Pick local folders and keep each knowledge base tied to its own repository state.
desktop_teaser_download_text: Desktop installers and Android sideloading are first-class; store distribution is not the default path.
desktop_teaser_private_text: The app wraps Mimir Core instead of creating a second hosted index or cloud document store.
closing_title: Install Mimir, keep the dossier local, query the evidence.
closing_text: No hosted account is required. The desktop client will arrive later; the open-source CLI and MCP layer are the product foundation now.
footer_text: Mimir is built by JCode Labs for sovereign local retrieval over confidential documents.
